content:
WHAT IS A
THESIS STATEMENT
A thesis statement is a sentence that captures the main point or claim of an essay or research paper. It serves as a roadmap, guiding the reader through the arguments that are presented in the work. Clear and concise, a thesis statement sets the tone and direction for the entire piece of writing.

(Source: https://www.examples.com/english/thesis-statement-example.htmlLinks to an external site.)

After you finish this page, click Next to continue to Writing a Strong Thesis Statement.


Week 4 | Writing a Strong Thesis Statement

Writing a Strong Thesis Statement
A strong thesis statement will typically include a clear and concise overview of the main points of your paper in the form of a strong statement. For example, suppose you are writing an essay on grade-level reading for children. In that case, you might claim: "Reading abilities in children is a problem that can be prevented with proper intervention and practice."

To craft a strong thesis statement, start by brainstorming ideas and narrowing your focus to one specific topic. Once you have decided on a topic, research to gather evidence to support your claims. Then, formulate your thesis statement as a clear and concise sentence that outlines your paper's central argument. Before submitting your paper, proofread your thesis statement for any errors.
